idea.topic: Monster Black Hole vs. Solar System
idea.hook: Our entire solar system? Not even close.
facts: The distance from our Sun to the heliopause (the edge of the Solar System's influence) is roughly 18 billion kilometers.
The supermassive black hole TON 618 has a mass equivalent to 66 BILLION Suns.
Its event horizon, the point of no return, measures an astounding 390 BILLION kilometers in diameter.
idea.payoff: Our entire Solar System would be a tiny speck, lost deep within TON 618's gravitational shadow.
idea.title: BLACK HOLE vs. Solar System 🤯

scenes.visual: A vibrant, rotating 3D model of the Solar System (Sun, planets, asteroid belt, Kuiper Belt) slowly zooms out.
The Solar System model continues to shrink, a faint blue line appears, marking the heliopause. The scale numbers '18 BILLION km' appear next to it.
Quick transition to a dark, swirling image of a supermassive black hole accretion disk. Numbers '66 BILLION SUNS' flash, illustrating immense mass.
The black hole image centers, with a glowing orange ring clearly defining the event horizon. A measurement line with '390 BILLION km' appears across it.

scenes.text: Our Solar System: A TINY SPECK, lost in TON 618.
scenes.visual: The Solar System from Scene 2 appears as an almost invisible, tiny pixel, then rapidly gets sucked into the vast darkness of TON 618's event horizon, disappearing entirely.
scenes.visual_details: The scale contrast is extreme. The tiny speck represents the entire 18-billion-km Solar System.
